summary: The diff implements both REQ-1 (light mode text visibility) and REQ-2 (dark mode teal accent scheme) through CSS variable changes in frontend/src/index.css and component-level Tailwind class updates. REQ-1 is partially addressed via CSS variables but has a notable gap: the light mode sidebar still uses dark backgrounds with light text rather than dark text on light backgrounds. REQ-2 appears well-implemented with teal CSS variables for dark mode primary/accent colors, sidebar active states, buttons, progress bars, and chart colors.

missing_requirements.requirement: CSS custom properties for sidebar text in the light theme use dark values — no white or near-white text on light backgrounds in light mode for sidebar
missing_requirements.reason: In frontend/src/index.css, the light mode :root defines --sidebar-background as oklch(0.12 0.02 240) (very dark) and --sidebar-foreground as oklch(0.92 0.01 240) (near-white). This means the sidebar in light mode has a dark background with light/white text, rather than the required dark text on a light/white background. The acceptance criteria explicitly requires sidebar text CSS properties to use dark values in the light theme.
